statement: In large native C++ repositories, keep always-on instructions short and move language, framework, and subsystem rules into scoped .instructions.md files plus skills so high-signal native guidance appears only when relevant.
publicSummary: Large C++ repos benefit from layered instructions because native-code rules are numerous, specific, and expensive to keep in always-on context.
cautionNotes: Keep the scoped files concrete; do not duplicate the same prose across always-on instructions, scoped instructions, and skills.
nonGeneralizationNotes: Do not encode private subsystem names or internal architecture details in the public conclusion.
